subject: ap-chemistry
title: Buffer Solutions & Acid-Base Equilibrium
stimulus:
4. A student is asked to prepare a buffer solution made with equimolar amounts of CH NH (aq) and CH NH Cl(s).
3 2 3 3
The student uses 25.00 mL of 0.100 M CH NH (aq), which contains 0.00250 mol of CH NH , to make the
3 2 3 2
buffer.

parts.prompt:
Calculate the mass of CH NH Cl(s) that contains 0.00250 mol of CH NH Cl.
3 3 3 3
The student has the following materials and equipment available.
• Distilled water • Electronic balance • 50 mL beaker • Pipets
• 0.100 M CH NH (aq) • Weighing paper • 10.0 mL graduated cylinder • pH meter
• Solid CH N H Cl • 50.00 mL buret • Small spatula
3 3

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value: 1 point

parts.prompt:
The following table contains a partial procedure for making the buffer solution. Fill in steps 1 and 4 to
complete the procedure using only materials and equipment selected from the choices given. (Not all materials
listed will be used. Assume that all appropriate safety measures are already in place.)
Step Procedure
2 Place the solid in the 50 mL beaker.
3 Clean the buret and rinse with distilled water.
5 Use the buret to add 25.00 mL of 0.100 M CH NH ( aq) to the beaker.
6 Mix well.
7 Check the pH with the pH meter.

The value of K for CH NH (aq) is 4.4 × 10$^{-4}$ , and the pH of the buffer the student prepared is 10.64.
b 3 2

scoring_points.description: For a correct description of step 1: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives
alternatives.correct_example: Use the spatula, balance, and weighing paper to measure out exactly 0.169 g of 33CH NH Cl( ) s

scoring_points.description: For a correct description of step 4: 1 point

required_elements: For a correct description of step 4: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: Rinse the buret with a small amount of 0.100 M CH NH (aq), drain, and refill with

parts.prompt:
The student prepares a second buffer solution. The student uses 25.00 mL of 0.050 M CH NH (aq) instead
of 25.00 mL of 0.100 M CH NH (aq), and half the mass of CH NH Cl(s) that was used in the first buffer. Is
3 2 3 3
the pH of the second buffer greater than, less than, or equal to the pH of the first buffer? Justify your answer.
Unauthorized copying or reuse of this page is illegal. Page 13

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: Equal to. The ratio of weak acid to conjugate base is still 1:1.
